parts.answer: the mountain bikers, their equipment and their activities in paragraph 2, beginning ‘Downhill mountain biking has become «’ The general effect is of many devoted bikers going to great effort to enjoy an adrenalin-pumping experience which stays with them even into the night. downhill aficionados: mountain biking devotees, passionate, slightly humorous lug: heave, drag with considerable effort lavishly suspensioned machines: extravagant levels of suspension, luxurious, overdone, no expense spared, complex technical design network (of chair lifts): complex system, connected, spreading out across area, coordinated, planned inching and slipping « like cautious baby penguins (at the head of the ice skid) (image): careful, initially small, tentative movements; novice-like; a group of vulnerable figures huddled together, one taking confidence from another succumbing to the crazy dance of the drop: giving in / taken over; spontaneous / random / choreographed movements of sudden descent; madness of taking part manic speeds: ridiculously fast, insanely quick, loss of control punctuated by brake-juddering twists and turns: broken-up by very fast and frequent changes in direction; jarring, unpredictable, painful embroidered tales (of near catastrophe) (image): embellished stories, exaggerated claims, detailed and colourful, wanting to impress October/November Question Answer Marks

the sights in the region in paragraph 5, beginning ‘basking in a mild sense of accomplishment « ’ The general effect is of the silent magnificence, stillness and dignity of nature – in striking contrast to the noisy chaos of the day. bathed in the gentle violet hues of dusk (image): covered, surrounded with, soft early evening colours, comforting – striking contrast to the appearance in the day (the inclines, twists and drops took on a) softer presence: looked more inviting, less threatening (large, black conifer trees) crept noiselessly (image): number / size of trees growing on the mountainsides, moving silently, slowly and carefully, sneaking, not wishing to be noticed mild mockery (of the raucous day time chair lift users): humour at the expense of the mountain bikers who need the comfort and safety of chair lifts (snowy peaks) peeped (image): emerged, peered out, a slight or brief action modest affirmation: humble, unassuming confirmation, not boastful majestic beauty: impressive, spectacularly attractive, royalty, finery and magnificence October/November Marking Criteria for Question 2 Table A, Reading: Language analysis: Use the following table to give a mark out of 10 for Reading. Band 6: 9–10 • Wide ranging discussion of judiciously selected language with some high quality comments that add meaning and associations to words / phrases in both parts of the question, and demonstrate the writer's reasons for using them. • Tackles imagery with some precision and imagination. • There is clear evidence that the candidate understands how language works. Band 5: 7–8 • Explanations are given of carefully selected words and phrases. • Explanations of meanings within the context of the passage are secure and effects are identified in both parts of the question. • Images are recognised as such and the response goes some way to explaining them. • There is some evidence that the candidate understands how language works.
